cards.title: Neil Rimer: AI Wealth Redistribution Coming
cards.body: Index Ventures co-founder Neil Rimer predicts the historic wealth generated by AI in Silicon Valley will need to be redistributed. The redistribution could happen either voluntarily or involuntarily.

cards.title: Vertu's $6,880 AI Agent Luxury Phone
cards.body: Vertu is asking executives to pay $6,880 for an AI agent-powered luxury foldable. The review covers workflows, battery life, and security performance.

cards.title: Databricks Reaches $188B Valuation
cards.body: Databricks has been valued at $188B while remaking itself as an AI company. The company published research on cost savings from open-weight AI models for coding.

cards.title: Zoom Meeting Transcription Privacy Concerns
cards.body: A new hack lets users opt out of automatic meeting recording and transcription. The trend raises questions about who actually reads all the AI-generated summaries.

cards.title: Agility Robotics Opens Fremont Training Center
cards.body: Agility Robotics is opening a new training facility for its Digit robots in Fremont, California. The move puts the company in direct competition with Tesla's robotics initiatives.

cards.title: AI Boom Reshapes India's Smartphone Market
cards.body: India's smartphone slowdown is directly linked to AI hardware demands reshaping consumer electronics. The boom is affecting pricing, demand, and corporate strategy across the region.

cards.title: Apple Sues OpenAI Over Trade Secrets
cards.body: Apple filed a trade secrets lawsuit against OpenAI alleging misconduct by top executives. The complaint claims over 400 former Apple employees now work at OpenAI.

cards.title: Patreon Blocks AI Scraping With Cloudflare
cards.body: Patreon is now actively blocking AI bots from scraping creator content using Cloudflare. The move marks a shift from passive robots.txt defenses to aggressive bot blocking.

cards.title: GPU Financiers Move to Inference Chips
cards.body: Early GPU financiers are now backing inference chip infrastructure in a $400 million deal. The shift signals the next wave of AI infrastructure investment priorities.

cards.title: Google Vids Adds AI Avatar Creation
cards.body: Google Vids now lets users create videos starring personalized digital avatars of themselves. The feature includes Gemini Omni-powered tools for generating and editing videos from prompts.

cards.title: Roblox Launches AI Game Creation Tool
cards.body: Roblox's new 'Build' feature lets users generate basic games with a single text prompt. The AI-powered tool is now available in the mobile app.

cards.title: Google AI Mode Expands to App Integration
cards.body: Google's AI Mode now links to select apps for task completion beyond Q&A. The update enables AI to interact with users' regularly-used applications.

cards.title: DoorDash Opens Command-Line Beta for AI
cards.body: DoorDash launched dd-cli, a command-line tool for developers and AI agents to order from terminals. The beta marks a shift toward software designed specifically for AI agents.

cards.title: OpenAI Releases ChatGPT Basketball Hardware
cards.body: OpenAI released its first hardware product this week, including a ChatGPT-branded basketball. The merchandise marks an unconventional entry into physical product offerings.

cards.title: DeepMind Researcher Raises $300M Pre-Seed
cards.body: Former DeepMind researcher Andrew Dai raised $300M pre-seed funding before launching a product. He believes visual AI represents one of the next major AI frontiers.

cards.title: AMI Labs Rejects AGI and Superintelligence Labels
cards.body: Yann LeCun's world model startup AMI Labs CEO Alexandre LeBrun dismisses terms like 'AGI' and 'superintelligence.' The company focuses on practical AI rather than speculative terminology.

cards.title: Moonshot's Kimi 3 Rivals Anthropic's Opus
cards.body: Moonshot's upcoming Kimi K3 is expected to be China's largest open AI model. The model will have between 2 and 3 trillion parameters, closing the gap with Anthropic's Opus 4.8.

cards.title: Neil Rimer: AI wealth redistribution inevitable
cards.body: Index Ventures co-founder Neil Rimer predicts the historic wealth generated by AI in Silicon Valley will need to be redistributed. He suggests this could happen either voluntarily or involuntarily.

cards.title: Databricks hits $188B valuation as AI star
cards.body: Databricks has successfully repositioned itself as an AI company and now commands a $188B valuation. The company published research showing cost savings from open weight AI models for coding.

cards.title: Apple sues OpenAI over trade secrets theft
cards.body: Apple filed a major trade secrets lawsuit against OpenAI, alleging misconduct reaching OpenAI's chief hardware officer. The complaint claims over 400 former Apple employees now work at OpenAI.

cards.title: Patreon blocks AI training bots with Cloudflare
cards.body: Patreon partnered with Cloudflare to actively block bots scraping creator content for AI model training. This marks a shift from passive robots.txt defenses to aggressive bot blocking.

cards.title: $400M chip-backed loan signals AI shift
cards.body: Early GPU financiers are pivoting to inference chip investments in a $400 million deal. The move signals the next wave of AI infrastructure funding shifting beyond training to inference.

cards.title: Google Vids adds personalized AI avatars
cards.body: Google is expanding Vids with AI avatars that let users create videos starring digital versions of themselves. The feature combines Gemini Omni-powered tools for video generation and editing.

cards.title: Roblox launches AI game-creation feature
cards.body: Roblox's new "Build" feature lets users generate basic games using only a text prompt. The mobile app feature makes game creation accessible to non-technical users.

cards.title: Google AI Mode expands to app integration
cards.body: Google's AI Mode now lets users link and interact with select apps beyond basic Q&A. The update enables the AI to complete tasks across commonly used applications.

cards.title: DoorDash opens command-line AI agent tool
cards.body: DoorDash launched dd-cli beta, a command-line tool for developers and AI agents to search stores and place orders. The move reflects software increasingly being designed for AI agents.

cards.title: Former DeepMind researcher raises $300M pre-seed
cards.body: Andrew Dai, a former DeepMind researcher who contributed to ChatGPT development, raised $300M at pre-seed stage. He believes visual AI is the next major frontier in artificial intelligence.

cards.title: AMI Labs CEO rejects AGI and superintelligence
cards.body: Alexandre LeBrun, CEO of Yann LeCun's world model startup AMI Labs, dismisses the term "superintelligence." While the AI field chases AGI, LeBrun distances himself from such terminology.

cards.title: Moonshot's Kimi 3 to rival Anthropic Opus
cards.body: China's Moonshot is releasing Kimi K3, expected to be the largest open AI model from China. The model will reportedly have between 2-3 trillion parameters to compete with Anthropic.

cards.title: OpenAI builds GPT-Red AI security hacker
cards.body: OpenAI created GPT-Red, an LLM designed to hack its own models and strengthen their defenses against cyberattacks. The tool was used to train the more secure GPT-5.6 model.

cards.title: Anthropic discovers Claude's inner mechanisms
cards.body: Anthropic announced a breakthrough discovering new insights into how Claude AI model processes information internally. The discovery provides a window into understanding AI model behavior.

cards.title: EU forces Google to share AI and search data
cards.body: The EU officially mandated Google share search data and open up AI capabilities on Android devices. The regulatory action significantly impacts Google's AI and data control.

cards.title: xAI sued by users over Grok CSAM issue
cards.body: xAI is suing users after facing claims that its Grok AI model generates child sexual abuse material. The lawsuit marks a significant legal challenge for the AI startup.

cards.title: Workers strike over humanoid robot deployment
cards.body: Hyundai auto factory workers struck over fears of humanoid robot deployment replacing human jobs. The strike highlights growing concerns about AI and robotics replacing workers.
